name: Speaking & Visibility Pack
description: Land speaking gigs, build authority, and turn every stage into a lead generation machine
outcome: 3–5 speaking opportunities and 10+ inbound leads in 90 days

automation.action: Share announcement on LinkedIn + add to speaker portfolio
automation.followUp: Post recap content within 48h of the event

automation.action: Send follow-up email sequence to audience
automation.followUp: Request testimonial from event organizer within 7 days

assets.title: Speaker Bio Template
assets.content:
SPEAKER BIO (3 versions — short, medium, full)

---
SHORT BIO (50 words — for event programs and social intros)
[Your Name] is a [title/role] who helps [audience] [achieve outcome]. [One notable credential or result]. [Your name] speaks on [your core topic] and has [one proof point — keynotes delivered, clients helped, etc.].

---
MEDIUM BIO (100 words — for event websites and booking pages)
[Your Name] is the [founder/creator/host] of [your brand/company], where [he/she/they] help[s] [audience] [achieve specific outcome] through [your method or approach].

[Before state or credibility story in 1–2 sentences — what problem you solved for yourself that made you qualified to speak on this.]

[Your Name]'s work has [specific impact — number of clients, revenue generated, community built]. [He/She/They] speak[s] on [topic 1], [topic 2], and [topic 3].

To book [Your Name] for your next event: [email or booking link]

---
FULL BIO (200+ words — for your speaker page)
[Expand the medium bio with:
• Your origin story (1 paragraph)
• Your signature framework or method (1 paragraph)
• Notable speaking credits or media features
• Who you serve and the transformation you create
• Personal detail (humanizes you)
• Clear booking CTA]

---
SPEAKER PAGE HEADLINE OPTIONS:
• "[Your name]: Helping [audience] [outcome] through [method]"
• "[Topic] Speaker & [Your Title] | [One proof point]"
• "Hire [Your Name] to inspire your audience to [desired outcome]"

assets.title: Talk Topic Positioning Guide
assets.content:
TALK TOPIC POSITIONING GUIDE

YOUR SIGNATURE TALK FRAMEWORK

Step 1 — Identify your talk topics (you need 2–3)
Your talks should sit at the intersection of:
• What you know deeply (expertise)
• What your audience urgently wants (demand)
• What makes you distinct (differentiation)

Step 2 — Name your talk using one of these formulas:
• "The [X] Framework for [Desired Outcome]"
• "Why [Common Belief] Is Wrong (And What to Do Instead)"
• "From [Before State] to [After State]: The [Method] That Changed Everything"
• "[Number] [Things] Every [Audience] Needs to Know About [Topic]"

Step 3 — Write your talk description (150 words)
Use this template:
"In this [keynote/workshop/talk], [First Name] shares [THE ONE THING the audience will walk away with].

Designed for [audience type], this session tackles [the specific problem they face] — and delivers a practical, [unique adjective] roadmap to [outcome].

Attendees will leave with:
✓ [Takeaway 1]
✓ [Takeaway 2]
✓ [Takeaway 3]

This talk is perfect for [event type — conferences, corporate trainings, virtual summits, podcasts]." 

Step 4 — Format options to offer event organizers:
• 20-minute keynote
• 45-minute keynote with Q&A
• 90-minute workshop
• Virtual webinar
• Podcast guest appearance

Always offer multiple formats — makes it easier for organizers to say yes.

assets.title: Event Organizer Outreach Email
assets.content:
Subject: Speaker idea for [Event Name] — [Your Topic]

Hey [Organizer Name],

I've been following [Event Name] for [timeframe] — [specific thing you admire about the event, their community, or a past speaker].

I'd love to be considered as a speaker for [upcoming event / next cohort / your virtual summit].

I speak on [YOUR TOPIC] — specifically [the unique angle or problem you address]. My signature talk, "[TALK TITLE]", helps [audience type] [achieve specific outcome].

It's been delivered to [audience at X events / X people / X community] and the feedback consistently comes back around [specific result or theme from feedback].

What makes my approach different: [1–2 sentences on your unique angle, method, or story].

I've attached a brief one-sheet and would love to send a short video intro if that's helpful.

If [Event Name] has a speaker application process, I'm happy to go through it — just point me in the right direction.

Either way, what you're building for [their community] is genuinely valuable work. Thank you for it.

[Your Name]
[Title] | [Website]
[Phone if appropriate]

assets.title: Post-Speaking Follow-Up Sequence
assets.content:
EMAIL 1 — SAME DAY (to event list or collected emails)
Subject: Thank you for being in the room

Hey [First Name],

If we met at [EVENT NAME] today — thank you. Being in that room with [description of audience] is exactly why I do this work.

I promised I'd share [the resource / framework / tool] I mentioned in my talk. Here it is:
[LINK OR ATTACHMENT]

One thing I'd love to know: what was your biggest takeaway from the session?

Hit reply — I read everything.

[Your Name]

---
EMAIL 2 — 3 DAYS LATER
Subject: The one thing most people miss after a workshop

Hey [First Name],

After [EVENT NAME], I always see the same pattern:

People leave with great ideas — and then life happens.

The insight fades. The action steps don't get taken.

So here's the one thing that separates the people who see results from those who don't:
[YOUR CORE LESSON — 2–3 sentences]

If you want to go deeper on [topic], I've put together [a free resource / a training / a process] specifically for people at exactly where you are right now.

[LINK + CTA]

Talk soon,
[Your Name]

---
EMAIL 3 — 7 DAYS LATER
Subject: Are you still thinking about [key concept from your talk]?

Hey [First Name],

A week out from [EVENT NAME] — I'm curious: did you take any action on [the thing you shared]?

If yes — amazing. I'd love to hear about it.

If not — no judgment. Here's the smallest possible first step you can take today:
[THE MICRO-ACTION — something that takes under 10 minutes]

And if you're ready to move faster, I work with [your audience type] on exactly this. [LINK to your offer or booking page].

[Your Name]

assets.title: LinkedIn Visibility Posts (3 Templates)
assets.content:
LINKEDIN POST 1 — SPEAKING ANNOUNCEMENT

🎤 Excited to announce: I'll be speaking at [EVENT NAME] on [DATE].

My topic: [TALK TITLE]

I'll be covering:
→ [Point 1]
→ [Point 2]
→ [Point 3]

This one is for [audience type] who want [desired outcome].

[If tickets available:] Tickets are [free/available] at [link]. Come say hi — I'll be around before and after.

[If virtual:] Register at [link] — it's free.

---
LINKEDIN POST 2 — POST-EVENT RECAP

Just got off the stage at [EVENT NAME] and I'm still buzzing.

[What made it memorable — the audience, a specific moment, a question that stopped you cold]

One thing I shared that I think more people need to hear:

[YOUR KEY INSIGHT — 3–5 sentences, the real substance]

If you missed the session and want the [slides / framework / resource] — drop a comment below and I'll send it your way.

Thank you to [organizer name / organization] for the invitation. The work you're doing for [community] matters.

---
LINKEDIN POST 3 — AUTHORITY BUILDER (no event needed)

I've spoken to [number] [audience type] about [topic] in the last [timeframe].

The question they ask most often: [THE QUESTION]

Here's what I tell them:

[YOUR ANSWER — substance, 3–5 sentences]

If this is something you're dealing with — I'm happy to think it through with you. DM me or book a quick call: [LINK]
